subject: DevOps
title: Secrets Management in Pipelines
description: A quick, practical guide to Secrets Management in Pipelines.

hook.text: A developer committed an AWS key to Git. A bot found it in 12 seconds and started mining crypto.

buildup.text: Pipelines need secrets — API keys, passwords, tokens — but they must never live in the code or YAML.

discovery.text: Secrets go in a vault. The pipeline pulls them at runtime. They appear as environment variables, never in logs.

twist.text: Good CI tools mask secrets in output. Even if a script prints them, the log shows asterisks.

climax.text: HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, and GitHub Secrets are standard tools. Rotate keys regularly.

punchline.text: Never commit secrets. Inject them at runtime from a vault.

quiz.question: How should CI/CD pipelines handle secrets like API keys?
options: Inject them at runtime from a secrets vault
Commit them in a private Git repository
Hardcode them in the pipeline YAML file
